title: Watch: Video shows how Iran navy forces seized ships in Hormuz amid US standoff
content: Iran released footage showing its forces seizing two commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, with the IRGC claiming the vessels were diverted to Iranian waters over alleged security violations. Meanwhile, Panama condemned the seizure of the MSC Francesca as “illegal,” warning it poses a serious threat to maritime security. Tehran said the IRGC captured the MSC Francesca and EPAMINODES in the Strait of Hormuz, alleging navigational interference and unauthorised movement.

title: Mitchell Starc set to join Delhi Capitals; selection headache on the cards
content: Mitchell Starc is set to join Delhi Capitals for IPL 2026 on Friday after Cricket Australia clearances, targeting a May 1 return against Rajasthan Royals. The injured fast bowler's arrival presents a selection dilemma for the Axar Patel-led side, which needs strong performances in upcoming matches to boost playoff hopes. Starc's gradual return will be managed carefully.

title: Starbucks layoffs hit company's technology teams; company sends memo to staff
content: Starbucks is reportedly cutting jobs within its technology teams as part of a significant restructuring. This move aims to sharpen focus and reallocate resources effectively under new CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround plan. These layoffs follow previous workforce reductions and store closures, signaling a broader effort to streamline operations and address financial pressures.

title: 'Raped another woman day before': Killer knew IRS officer's routine, timed entry to assault daughter
content: A 23-year-old man, accused of a brutal rape and murder in Delhi, allegedly committed another heinous crime in Alwar, Rajasthan, hours prior. He forced his way into a woman's home, raped and attempted to strangle her before fleeing. Police apprehended him in a Delhi hotel after a multi-state manhunt.

title: Boston Marathon: Runner gives up personal best to save collapsing competitor
content: Sportsmanship shone at the Boston Marathon 2026 when two runners, Aaron Beggs and Robson de Oliveira, stopped to help a struggling first-time participant, Ajay Haridasse, just meters from the finish line. Despite sacrificing personal bests, they supported him across the line. All three later met qualifying standards for the next marathon.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: IPL 2026: RR bowler fined for send-off gesture to Rishabh Pant
content: Rajasthan Royals pacer Nandre Burger has been fined 10% of his match fees and received one demerit point for a send-off gesture towards Rishabh Pant. This disciplinary action occurred during RR's dominant 40-run victory over LSG, where their bowling attack restricted the opposition to 119.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' box office Day 6: Akshay Kumar starrer crosses Rs 127 crore globally
content: Akshay Kumar's latest horror-comedy, 'Bhooth Bangla', is making waves at the box office, raking in over Rs 100 crore globally within its first four days. Although it faced a slight decline during the weekdays, the film still attracted audiences, collecting Rs 6.15 crore on Day 6 in India and pushing its total net earnings to Rs 78.90 crore. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: 1 lakh fake ENO, 50k coffee sachets seized: FSSAI shares 2 simple purity tests
content: Authorities have busted two illegal manufacturing units in Delhi, seizing over 1.5 lakh counterfeit sachets of popular brands like ENO and Nescafé. This crackdown highlights the growing problem of fake and adulterated food products in India, which poses a significant risk to consumers. The report also offers simple methods to detect common coffee adulterants like clay and chicory powder.

title: Denmark rail accident: Two trains collide head-on near Copenhagen, several injured
content: A severe head-on collision between two local trains in Denmark on Thursday has left several passengers injured. The incident, occurring near Copenhagen, prompted a large-scale emergency response. Authorities confirmed all passengers were safely evacuated, with no one trapped. Investigations into the cause of the major accident are underway.

title: ‘Bush, Obama said no’: Trump agreed to Netanyahu's war plan after rejections by 3 US Presidents
content: John Kerry revealed that Benjamin Netanyahu pitched an Iran war to four US Presidents, with only Donald Trump agreeing. Kerry, citing Vietnam and Iraq, warned against misleading the public on military action. A New York Times report detailed Netanyahu's "hard sell" to Trump for a joint US-Israeli mission, which Trump reportedly approved.

title: How long will it take for US to clear Strait of Hormuz mines positioned by Iran?
content: Iran's alleged mining of the Strait of Hormuz could delay its reopening for up to six months, according to Pentagon reports to Congress. This sophisticated deployment, using GPS technology, is making detection difficult for US forces. The standoff has already impacted global oil prices, with Brent crude exceeding $100 per barrel.  US officials vow to counter this threat.

title: Watch | ‘Dimaag me kuch nahi chalta’: Sooryavanshi reveals his pre-match routine
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to reach 500 IPL runs, achieving the milestone in just 227 balls. Despite a quiet 8 runs against Lucknow Super Giants, his record-breaking feat and admission of watching cartoons before matches captivated fans. Rajasthan Royals secured a 40-run victory, with Ravindra Jadeja earning Player of the Match.

title: Will gold head to Rs 1.63 lakh/10 grams & silver hit Rs 2.80 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices are showing sideways-to-bullish momentum, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: Asian stocks today: Markets retreat after record-high due to oil surge, Iran tensions weigh
content: Asian markets faltered after touching record highs, weighed down by surging oil prices and lingering US-Iran peace talk uncertainties. Japan's Nikkei briefly crossed 60,000 before retreating. Escalating Middle East tensions, including shipping disruptions, fueled oil price spikes, impacting investor sentiment across the region. Despite a Wall Street rally, regional markets succumbed to geopolitical anxieties.

title: ‘Mischievous & misleading’: Govt dismisses ‘fake news’ on petrol, diesel price hike
content: The ministry of petroleum and natural gas has firmly refuted claims of an impending petrol and diesel price hike, labeling such reports as 'fake news' designed to cause public alarm.  Asserting India's unique fuel price stability over the past four years, the government highlighted its proactive measures to shield citizens from global energy market volatility and geopolitical impacts.

title: ‘I don’t have answers… no excuses’: Rishabh Pant after LSG’s crushing defeat
content: Rishabh Pant expressed deep disappointment after Lucknow Super Giants' batting collapse, admitting his team "had no answers" in their 40-run IPL 2026 defeat. He called for introspection, stating the batting unit, including himself, failed to adapt to the challenging pitch.  Rajasthan Royals, anchored by Ravindra Jadeja's unbeaten 43, successfully defended their modest total.

title: ISI tactic? Punjab woman becomes 'Sarabnoor', invites Sikhs to visit Pakistan in video
content: Sarabjit Kaur, who converted to Islam and married a Pakistani man, has released a new video from Pakistan inviting Sikhs to visit the country. This conciliatory message follows earlier provocative remarks, raising suspicions among Indian intelligence agencies who believe she may be acting under ISI influence to gauge Sikh public reaction.

title: Kollywood stars cast votes: Rajinikanth, Vijay, and Ajith lead early polling push
content: The team behind Suriya's upcoming film 'Karuppu' has arrived in Sri Lanka. They received a grand welcome. The cast and crew are there for film promotions and to complete shooting. This overseas schedule is generating significant buzz. The movie is set for a May 14 release. Expectations are high for this RJ Balaji directorial.

title: UP man strangled by wife, children over fear of losing daughter’s Rs 82.5k wedding fund
content: A man was allegedly murdered by his wife, daughters, and minor son in Mahoba district. The family feared he would misuse Rs 82,500 arranged for his daughter's wedding. The body was found in a drain. The accused confessed to strangling him and dumping the body. Police recovered the murder weapon, cash, and a damaged mobile phone.

title: Explained: Why China’s AI computing power looks 6,000x bigger
content: China reports a staggering 1,882 exaflops of AI computing power, vastly exceeding global benchmarks and raising questions about hidden capacity. While metrics differ, adjusted figures still show significant advancement. This development, coupled with China's withdrawal from global rankings amid geopolitical tensions, suggests a rapidly narrowing gap with the US in AI infrastructure and capability.

title: Who was Amal Khalil? Lebanese journalist killed as Israel–Hezbollah tensions escalate
content: A Lebanese journalist, Amal Khalil, was tragically killed in an Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon while covering the ongoing conflict. Rescue efforts were significantly delayed by Israeli fire, preventing immediate access to the site. Khalil's death, the ninth journalist killed this year, has drawn condemnation and calls for international law adherence.

title: Viral 7-day diet plan: Know the weekly meals that helped fitness influencer lose 20 kgs
content: Weight loss advice in 2026 is surprisingly simple. A viral 7-day Indian meal plan focuses on whole foods and portion control. This realistic approach is gaining attention for its balance and consistency. Scientific research supports each day's nutritional logic. The plan emphasises everyday home-cooked meals and sustainable habits over extreme diets. It offers a doable strategy for long-term success.

title: BCCI pushes for Jasprit Bumrah to play entire WTC cycle
content: Following their T20 World Cup win, selectors are prioritising the World Test Championship and 2027 ODI World Cup, focusing on managing Jasprit Bumrah's workload. Bumrah is expected to play all nine upcoming WTC Tests, with potential rest from ODIs. Middle-order prospects like Padikkal, Jurel, and Gaikwad are also being considered.

title: ‘Raped another woman in Rajasthan’: Chilling details emerge in IRS officer’s daughter murder case
content: A 22-year-old daughter of a senior bureaucrat was allegedly raped and murdered at her south Delhi residence. Police arrested a 23-year-old former domestic help within 12 hours, who was dismissed for financial misconduct. He is accused of robbery, sexual assault, and murder, and is also wanted for a similar crime in Rajasthan.

title: Youngest and fastest! Sooryavanshi scripts history, breaks two major IPL records
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made history by becoming the youngest player to reach 500 Indian Premier League runs and the fastest by balls faced. Despite a quiet 8 runs, his achievement came during Rajasthan Royals' 40-run victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Ravindra Jadeja's all-round performance secured the win for RR.

title: Pentagon shake-up continues: US navy secretary steps down abruptly amid war with Iran
content: US Navy secretary John Phelan has abruptly resigned, with Hung Cao stepping in as acting chief. This departure occurs amidst heightened regional tensions, including the Navy's blockade of Iranian ports and ongoing operations in critical waterways. Phelan's exit is the latest in a series of significant leadership changes within the Pentagon.

title: 'Playing at Perth': Justin Langer compares Ekana to WACA as LSG struggle at home
content: Lucknow Super Giants coach Justin Langer admits his team struggles with the Ekana Stadium's pace and bounce, comparing it to Perth's WACA.  Despite a brilliant surface, LSG batters haven't adapted, leading to three home losses. Langer praised his bowlers, highlighting Mohsin Khan and Prince Yadav as future India stars.

title: Oil prices today: Crude edges lower as Iran–US tensions keep markets on edge
content: Oil prices saw a slight dip after Wednesday's surge, as stalled Iran-US peace talks and ongoing shipping route restrictions, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, kept markets on edge. Iran's seizure of two ships and US interception of tankers highlight escalating tensions. Despite record US energy exports, domestic crude inventories unexpectedly rose, while fuel stocks declined.

title: West Bengal Elections 1st Phase Voting LIVE Updates | "Your Intervention Is Imperative": Congress' Adhir Ranjan Dials Poll Official After EVM Glitch
content: West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 1 Voting LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of the state's 294 constituencies are expected to vote today.

content: The non-oil deficit remains deeply negative. This means Russia still relies heavily on energy exports to balance books.

content: In Bengal, the number of voters has reduced by around 91 lakh after the Special Intensive Review (SIR).

content: Rahul Meena tried to overpower the girl by strangling her. When she resisted, Meena attacked her with a lamp and heavy object found nearby.

content: The victim, while recording her statement before the Women's Commission, said she was repeatedly subjected to mental harassment, inappropriate behaviour and conversations since her training days by one of the accused - Raza Memon.

content: Those urging revival want India to remember the treaty's durability. India is entitled to remember what it had to absorb to keep the treaty alive.

title: Trinamool-BJP Battle Royale In Bengal With 152 Seats In Phase 1
content: Two states that have eluded the BJP for long are voting today and more than Tamil Nadu, it is Bengal that all eyes will be on. For Trinamool Congress and the BJP, it is a desperate battle amid the political firestorm over voter list revision.

title: Days After US Woman Assaulted Karnataka Homestay's Licence Cancelled
content: Bengaluru, April 23 (IANS) In view of the sexual assault of an American woman tourist in Madikeri district, the Tourism Department on Thursday issued an order cancelling the licence of the homestay where the incident occurred.

title: Will Strait Of Hormuz Open This Year? What Report Said
content: The Pentagon's assessment points to potentially long-lasting economic consequences, as the critical trade artery carried a fifth of the world's oil during peacetime.

content: The accused, Rahul Meen, changed clothes and allegedly stole around Rs 2.5 lakh before fleeing the crime spot.

title: Voter Intimidation Charge, Trinamool vs Humayun Kabir As Bengal Votes
content: Allegations of voter intimidation have surfaced from Murshidabad and Trinamool workers clashed with Humayun Kabir in the same district as West Bengal votes in phase 1.

title: FBI Investigated Reporter Who Wrote Article About Kash Patel's Girlfriend
content: NYT's Elizabeth Williamson wrote that FBI chief Kash Patel had assigned four agents to protect Alexis Wilkins full time.

title: DMK vs AIADMK, And A Vijay 'Hero Or Cameo' Question As Tamil Nadu Votes
content: Tamil Nadu is voting today for the assembly elections, with the high-stakes contest pitting the DMK alliance against the AIADMK-led NDA. MK Stalin, whose campaign centred around the "Dravidian Model" of governance, is seeking to retain power.

title: Teen Gives Birth Inside Foxconn Toilet In Bengaluru, Slits Baby's Throat
content: Renuka, an employee at the Foxconn factory in Devanahalli, delivered the baby inside a toilet and then slit his throat and dumped the body in a bag.

content: The India-Africa relationship is rooted in “our civilisational linkages”, nurtured over centuries through cultural exchanges and human interactions, the External Affairs Minister said
